propositions: Rising-floor remediation eligibility requires a prior CERTIFIED plan-review signature, so BLOCK loops (which never sign) can never qualify, making the floor structurally inert exactly where advisory walls balloon.
build_payload gains a repo_root keyword and always stamps two additive top-level sidecar fields, verified_at_sha and regver, self-sourced at emit time identically on PASS and BLOCK verdicts.
verified_at_sha resolves via current_code_sha() with a git-HEAD subprocess fallback so local-mode BLOCK loops (where current_code_sha() is None) still get a working baseline.

remediation_mode_candidate is rekeyed into two branches: an unchanged signature path when a valid plan-review manifest exists, and a sidecar fallback using the most recent REVIEW_RESULT sidecar otherwise.
Signature verification errors and non-plan-review manifests now fall through to the sidecar branch instead of returning ineligible; all other read errors remain fail-safe to full review.
The sidecar branch's decision dict uses exactly five reason keys and only becomes eligible when a prior sidecar carries all three baseline fields, avoiding a structurally-inert always-False result.
